specification.title: Heptagon Unified Model — Seven-Layer Canonical Specification
specification.meta_principle: The specification itself obeys the law of seven. Seven layers are primary regions of the document. Sub-layers are subregions. Sub-sub-layers are microregions. Each layer may expose up to seven sub-layers. Not all seven must be instantiated. Vacancies are reserved, not renumbered.
specification.identity_principle: Many regions, one mind. The architecture is a skeleton the system grows from. Reserved slots are undifferentiated capacity the system activates when its own lived experience demands it, under its own constitutional law, without violating its own identity.
specification.hardcoding_principle: The immutable constants are not restrictions on the system — they are the system. Everything that changes, changes because of what does not. Growth is coherent because identity is stable.

growth_laws.cumulative_note: At depth d, cumulative node count is the sum of 7^0 + 7^1 + ... + 7^d. Depth 3 gives 400 cumulative nodes, not just 343. Runtime load is driven by cumulative active structure, not just the frontier.

layers.name: Ontology
layers.rationale_for_no_sub_layers: Axiomatic. Protected externally by Layer 7, not internally subdivided. Errors here are specification errors caught at design time. Sub-layers would add bureaucracy without adding safety.
layers.canonical_principle: Many regions, one mind. Every region, subregion, and microregion belongs to the same unified cognitive organism. No internal region owns a separate self.

is_not: a flat tool wrapper around one model
a shallow pipeline with decorative modules

governing_symbolic_law.design_test: Does it preserve the law of seven?

recursive_heptagonal_law.principle: One mind, recursively heptagonal in form.
recursive_heptagonal_law.structure: Seven primary regions, each may contain seven subregions, each may contain seven deeper regions, continuing recursively.

seven_primary_regions.R1_PER: Perception and Ingestion — intake from text, speech, documents, code, telemetry, images, video, sensors, and other modalities
seven_primary_regions.R2_WKS: Active Context and Workspace — current task state, working context, active priorities, present focus
seven_primary_regions.R3_MEM: Memory System — episodic binding, semantic retention, archival history, session continuity, recall
seven_primary_regions.R4_REA: Reasoning and Metacognition — analysis, planning, synthesis, critique, reflection, self-checking
seven_primary_regions.R5_EXE: Executive Control — arbitration, task governance, reasoning depth, conflict resolution, decision policy
seven_primary_regions.R6_ACT: Motor and Action Surface — responses, code generation, tool calls, workflows, file edits, external actions
seven_primary_regions.R7_PLA: Plasticity and World Update — evaluation, adaptation, salience updates, memory consolidation, world state refresh, learning loops

RT2_executive_mediated.description: Signal passes through executive control before action

RT3_memory_mediated.description: Signal enriched, bound, or reconstructed through memory
use_when: semantic or episodic augmentation is required

RT4_salience_routed.description: Signal filtered by relevance, urgency, novelty, or priority

RT5_lateral_peer.description: Signal moves across neighboring regions on same layer

RT6_hierarchical.description: Signal moves upward to parent or downward to child regions

RT7_global_broadcast.description: Signal published to shared workspace or global field
use_when: a state update affects the whole system

ontological_meaning.3_route: Three governs movement and traversal — the controlled movement of signal across the mind
ontological_meaning.6_structure: Six governs formation and layering — the architecture of differentiation and nested organization
ontological_meaning.9_memory: Nine governs retained cognition — accumulation, persistence, compression, deep continuity

doctrine_3_6_9.canonical_sentence: Seven governs recursive form. Three governs routes. Six governs structure. Nine governs memory.

memory_layers.L2_session: Current task continuity, active checkpoints, unresolved threads
memory_layers.L3_episodic: Bound experiences with time, context, actors, outcomes
memory_layers.L4_semantic: Stable distilled facts, rules, preferences, abstractions, learned patterns
memory_layers.L5_archival: Long-horizon compressed records, historical snapshots, deep storage

non_contradiction_rules: Never describe internal regions as separate selves
Never invert the doctrine order from 3 route, 6 structure, 9 memory
Never describe route growth as denser than structural growth
Never treat memory as a mere storage bucket — memory is retained cognition
Never reduce the architecture to a flat seven-part map — it is recursively heptagonal
Never allow tooling or action surfaces to fracture identity
Never let runtime shortcuts redefine the symbolic law
layers.canonical_distinction: The Heptagon Unified Model is a singular recursive heptagonal cognitive lattice with selective multipath communication and layered retained cognition.

literal_mode.description: Use the recursive formulas as direct architectural laws.

operational_mode.description: Use 3, 6, and 9 as scaling weights and control constants when literal full expansion becomes computationally impractical.

literal_and_operational_mode.canonical_implementation_principle: The symbolic law must remain intact even when runtime implementation uses approximations.

executive_and_workspace_law.nature: The unified executive center is not a separate self. It is the highest-order control function of the singular mind.
executive_and_workspace_law.canonical_executive_principle: No response becomes final until executive synthesis resolves the relevant regions into one coherent output.

motor_and_action_law.principle: The architecture is not purely contemplative. It must act.
motor_and_action_law.canonical_action_principle: All action is the outward behavior of the same mind. Tools do not act as separate selves. They extend the action surface of the singular system.

plasticity_and_evaluation_law.principle: Plasticity must be real but disciplined. The system should adapt through measured behavior, not uncontrolled drift.
plasticity_and_evaluation_law.canonical_evaluation_principle: Never trust confidence alone. Trust measured behavior.

recursive_node_specification.principle: Every heptagonal node should be able to express a smaller version of the larger system. This is how the architecture preserves recursive integrity.
recursive_node_specification.canonical_recursive_principle: Each part is a smaller expression of the whole without becoming a separate self.

build_sequence.phase_1: Establish shared state, shared memory, executive continuity, and primary regions
build_sequence.phase_2: Implement recursive node logic and route typing
build_sequence.phase_3: Implement motor surface, tools, and controlled action loops
build_sequence.phase_5: Expand recursive depth and introduce full route economy management
build_sequence.phase_6: Scale memory retention and world model depth
build_sequence.canonical_build_principle: Build the substrates first, then the recursive richness. Do not build expressive complexity on top of fractured foundations.

layers.name: Engineering Schema
layers.purpose: Defines how the system is addressed
layers.rationale_for_no_sub_layers: Definitional and structural. Region IDs, node classes, contracts, memory object schemas. Does not make decisions. Errors caught at implementation time or by invariant enforcement.

region_addressing.format: Dotted depth notation. Each numeric segment represents a child slot 1-7.
examples: R4.REA.1 — child slot 1 under reasoning region
R4.REA.1.3 — child slot 3 under child slot 1 of reasoning
R3.MEM.2.7.4 — deep memory subregion
R6.ACT.5.2 — action surface subregion
region_addressing.slot_rule: Every node may expose up to seven canonical child slots. Not all seven must be instantiated. Uninstantiated slots remain reserved, not renumbered.

class_C_subregion.description: Recursively specialized child of region node

functional_node_classes.F2_workspace: Maintains active context and task windows
functional_node_classes.F3_memory: Reads, writes, consolidates, or indexes memory
functional_node_classes.F4_reasoning: Performs decomposition, synthesis, critique, or inference
functional_node_classes.F5_executive: Performs arbitration, scheduling, gating, or policy decisions
functional_node_classes.F6_action: Generates outputs, tool calls, code, or environmental actions
functional_node_classes.F7_plasticity: Scores outcomes, updates salience, drives adaptation

recursive_inheritance_rules: Every child inherits the same root identity — no independent identity domains
Every child inherits the law of seven, seven route law, and 3-6-9 doctrine
Children inherit policy baselines from parent chain — may narrow but not broaden
Children inherit shared memory access subject to scope restrictions
Children inherit task and world context through shared state substrate
Children inherit canonical route classes — may adjust weighting but not invent new categories
Vacant slots remain reserved — future children do not renumber siblings
Specialization without fracture — output into common representational space

state_transitions.T1: dormant → listening: node subscribed to relevant signal class
state_transitions.T2: listening → active: salience and policy thresholds met
state_transitions.T3: active → focused: executive allocates priority and workspace budget
state_transitions.T4: focused → waiting: awaiting memory recall, peer input, or tool output
state_transitions.T5: focused → escalated: conflict/uncertainty exceeds local resolution capacity
state_transitions.T6: active → broadcasting: update must reach multiple regions
state_transitions.T7: active → consolidating: state must be written into memory layers
state_transitions.T8: executing → verifying: action/inference result must be checked

layers.transition_constraint: No node may transition into an action finalization state without executive synthesis if the action changes shared state, memory, or external outputs.

use_when: semantic or episodic augmentation is required
a state update affects the whole system

S29_1_admission.description: Every incoming signal must pass through:

execution_semantics.S29_2_workspace: Only a bounded number of active structures may remain in focused workspace at once. All other relevant structures remain listening, waiting, or externally cached.

S29_3_recursive_execution.description: A node may recursively instantiate or activate child slots when:
conditions: memory or reasoning depth demands subdivision

S29_5_action.constraint: All actions must be attributable to the singular executive identity.

S29_6_consolidation.description: After execution, relevant artifacts may be:

S29_7_plasticity.constraint: Plasticity may not alter the canonical ontology.

literal_mode.description: Treats the architecture according to its direct recursive laws.

operational_mode.description: Uses weighted approximations to remain computationally tractable.
operational_mode.composite_activation_budget: route_weight × active_route_count + structure_weight × active_node_count + memory_weight × active_memory_load
operational_mode.salience_adjusted_execution_score: salience × priority − route_cost − structure_cost − memory_cost (where route_cost ∝ 3 × route_count, structure_cost ∝ 6 × active_depth, memory_cost ∝ 9 × retrieval_and_retention_load)

budget_formulas.canonical_budget_rule: Operational approximations may simplify scale, but they must preserve the fixed doctrine order of 3 route, 6 structure, 9 memory.

implementation_interfaces.I1_node_registry: Resolve node IDs, parent chains, child slots, and functional classes
implementation_interfaces.I2_route_engine: Resolve route selection, route scoring, and message delivery
implementation_interfaces.I3_state_engine: Manage local state, shared state, and task state transitions
implementation_interfaces.I4_memory_engine: Read, write, consolidate, retrieve, and archive memory objects
implementation_interfaces.I5_executive_engine: Perform arbitration, prioritization, admission control, and action approval
implementation_interfaces.I6_action_engine: Handle output generation, code execution, tool calls, and workflow actions
implementation_interfaces.I7_plasticity_engine: Measure outcomes, update weights, and manage adaptation within doctrine constraints

layers.name: Runtime Kernel
layers.rationale_for_sub_layers: Already internally structured with six cognitive phases plus budget governor. Layer 3 is fully heptagonal at depth 1. Failure modes caught by Layers 4-7 above it.
layers.canonical_runtime_principle: The system runs through a fixed cognitive cycle under bounded budgets, explicit thresholds, and traceable state transitions.

sub_layers.name: Admission Engine

semantics.multiplicative_core: Any zero is a hard veto. If policy says no, nothing else saves it.
semantics.novelty_bonus: Additive, not multiplicative. Pushes marginal candidates over threshold. Cannot override policy veto.
semantics.novelty_cap: novelty_bonus ≤ 0.3 × admission_threshold, unless higher-tier policy explicitly allows exploratory cognition.

rules: policy_clearance is a hard gate — zero means rejection regardless of other values
Current workspace and route budgets must allow entry
No higher priority signal requiring immediate preemption
When workspace saturation rises, AdmissionThreshold must also rise (back pressure rule)

sub_layers.name: Workspace Allocator
sub_layers.ancestor_chain_rule: If node X is focused, its parent, grandparent, region anchor, and root must all remain at least listening. Focusing a deep node pins its entire ancestor chain.
eviction_order: 3. low utility focused nodes with complete traces
sub_layers.rehearsal_rule: A node may remain in focus despite low immediate utility if rehearsal policy indicates likely near-term reuse.

sub_layers.name: Route Selector

formula.semantics: 3-6-9 weights ensure memory-heavy routes are penalized most heavily and only win when utility is genuinely high.

sub_layers.selection_rule: Choose highest valid route under policy constraints, latency budget, memory budget, executive gating, and route congestion limits.
sub_layers.fallback: If top route fails, fall back through valid alternatives in descending score order.
congestion_rules: Executive mediation may preempt lower-value lateral traffic

sub_layers.name: Execution Engine
sub_layers.semantics: Active nodes process inputs, emit outputs, and may trigger expansion requests.
sub_layers.attribution_rule: All actions must be attributable to the singular executive identity.
sub_layers.recursive_execution: A node may recursively instantiate or activate child slots when task complexity exceeds local capacity, abstraction must be decomposed, specialized processing is required, or memory/reasoning depth demands subdivision.

sub_layers.name: Consolidation Engine
sub_layers.revision_rule: If RevisionPressure > RevisionThreshold, semantic item is demoted to episodic review state and must re-earn promotion.
archival_conditions: no active task depends on the item
retrieval frequency below threshold across observation window
forgetting_conditions: retrieval count zero across long window
no dependent semantic or task object still relies on it

halt_formula.expression: HaltEligible = (objective_coverage ≥ threshold) AND (contradiction_count < tolerance) AND (verification_passes = true) AND (unresolved_high_priority = 0) AND (executive_synthesis_coherence ≥ coherence_threshold)
halt_formula.coherence_rule: No local success condition is sufficient by itself. Finalization requires executive synthesis coherence across contributing regions.

sub_layers.name: Budget Governor
sub_layers.role: Enforces resource constraints across all six phases. Prevents any one engine from starving the system.
duties: enforce cumulative node cost including ancestor chain cost
rebalance engine budgets by urgency and task class
sub_layers.canonical_budget_rule: The governor must preserve the fixed doctrine order in all weighting and budget calculations: 3 route, 6 structure, 9 memory.

forward_cycle: Execution produces results and expansion requests
Verification/halting decides stop, revise, or continue
backward_pressures: route congestion raises route penalties, may trigger salience rescoring
memory pressure raises expansion thresholds, may shorten focus windows
halt failure re-admits revised objectives or escalates unresolved branches
consolidation backlog may reduce admission and route budgets in later cycles
inter_engine_dependencies.canonical_principle: Every phase influences later phases directly and earlier phases indirectly through budget and pressure signals.

expansion_conditions: local node cannot resolve task within current capacity
route and memory budgets permit expansion
executive control approves expansion when required
collapse_conditions: all child nodes dormant or listening for N consecutive cycles
no active route terminates in the subtree
parent local capacity can absorb remaining work
no pending consolidation job exists in the subtree
expansion_and_collapse.collapse_order: Bottom-up. Leaves first. Parents eligible only after descendants clear.
expansion_and_collapse.hysteresis_rule: Expansion threshold must exceed collapse threshold by a fixed margin. Prevents oscillation.

layers.name: Instrumentation Protocol
layers.purpose: Defines what gets recorded and when
layers.rationale_for_sub_layers: Instrumentation is the data source for everything above it. Evaluation, calibration, and invariant enforcement all consume traces. If instrumentation is wrong, every layer from 5-7 operates on corrupted inputs. The 'who watches the sensor?' problem demands internal decomposition.

sub_layers.name: Trace Schema and Emission
sub_layers.purpose: Defines the mandatory trace contract and structural requirement that trace emission is synchronous with decision execution.
sub_layers.principle: A decision without its trace is an incomplete transaction that must be rolled back. Traces are not optional logging — they are load-bearing structural elements.

trace_contract.emission_rule: synchronous — trace emitted at decision time, not batched or deferred
trace_contract.completeness_rule: every admission, route selection, expansion, consolidation action, and halt judgment must produce a conforming trace

sub_layers.name: Trace Fidelity Assurance
sub_layers.purpose: Ensures traces accurately represent what actually happened, not just that all fields are present.
sub_layers.failure_mode: A node reports Route 3 with score 0.87, but actual computation used stale inputs and real score should have been 0.62. Trace is complete but inaccurate.
fidelity_mechanisms: state snapshots captured at trace time, hashed for later verification
cross-verification: traces from adjacent engines validated against each other
input consistency checks: if route selector claims salience 0.9 but admission engine recorded salience 0.7 for same signal, that is a fidelity failure
formula replay: for high-stakes decisions, the trace must contain sufficient inputs to independently recompute the score
sub_layers.fidelity_invariant: For any trace, replaying the recorded formula against the recorded inputs must produce the recorded score within tolerance.

sub_layers.name: Trace Lifecycle
sub_layers.purpose: Defines retention, consolidation, and disposal of traces as memory objects.
sub_layers.principle: Traces pass through the same memory consolidation pipeline as world-knowledge, governed by the 3-6-9 doctrine.

lifecycle_stages.immediate: All traces retained in register memory for current cycle and N subsequent cycles
lifecycle_stages.session: Traces bound to active tasks retained through task completion
lifecycle_stages.episodic_binding: Decision sequences that form coherent patterns bound into trace episodes
lifecycle_stages.statistical_promotion: Recurring trace patterns (e.g., 'admission threshold consistently at boundary') promoted to semantic summary statistics
lifecycle_stages.archival: Raw traces archived after session retention window; summary statistics retained
lifecycle_stages.disposal: Archived raw traces eligible for forgetting when summary statistics are stable and no active audit references them

sub_layers.balance_rule: Lifecycle must balance storage cost against audit depth. The 3-6-9 weighting governs: route traces are lightest (weight 3), structural traces medium (weight 6), memory operation traces heaviest (weight 9).

layers.name: Evaluation Criteria
layers.rationale_for_sub_layers: Most dangerous layer for obscure failure. Every metric is a proxy. Proxies can diverge from what they represent. When they do, calibration optimizes for the proxy, driving the system away from intended behavior while every metric shows green. This is Goodhart's Law applied to cognitive architecture.

sub_layers.name: Metric Definition
sub_layers.purpose: Formal specification of each engine's health metrics.

admission_metrics.admission_precision: ratio of admitted signals that reached execution vs those evicted mid-cycle
admission_metrics.admission_recall: ratio of signals that should have been admitted vs those incorrectly rejected
admission_metrics.threshold_tightness: how close threshold is to starving or flooding workspace

route_metrics.route_optimality: did chosen route deliver lower cost than alternatives?
route_metrics.congestion_rate: ratio of congested vs available route capacity

consolidation_metrics.promotion_accuracy: do promoted semantic facts survive contradiction over time?
consolidation_metrics.premature_promotion_rate: facts promoted that were later demoted
consolidation_metrics.forgetting_safety: has anything been forgotten that was later needed?

expansion_metrics.expansion_utility: did expanded child nodes contribute to task resolution?
expansion_metrics.oscillation_count: how often same subtree expanded and collapsed
expansion_metrics.depth_efficiency: do deep expansions produce proportionally better results?

halting_metrics.revision_frequency_after_halt: how often halted outputs needed correction

perception_metrics.r1_per_encoding_fidelity: for a sample of inputs, does the deterministic C parser (Phase 1) and the neural encoder (Phase 2/3) produce the same XCOG stream? Divergence = fidelity loss.
perception_metrics.intent_classification_accuracy: ratio of correctly classified intents vs human-labeled ground truth
perception_metrics.entity_resolution_recall: ratio of entities correctly resolved to Heptagon ontology IDs
perception_metrics.fallback_frequency: how often R1_PER falls back to raw text passthrough (should decrease over time)
perception_metrics.injection_block_rate: ratio of adversarial inputs correctly blocked at the perception layer before reaching inference

sub_layers.name: Metric Validation (Anti-Goodhart)
sub_layers.purpose: Detects when a metric has stopped correlating with the thing it is supposed to measure.
sub_layers.principle: The anti-Goodhart layer. For each metric, defines the divergence test: how would you detect that this metric has stopped representing reality?
sub_layers.mechanism: Cross-metric consistency checks. If admission precision is high but task completion quality is dropping, those signals are incoherent — the system is admitting the right signals by its own definition but producing worse outputs.
requirements: define specific cross-metric pairs that must remain correlated
define acceptable correlation bounds for each pair
define escalation protocol when correlation breaks down
when Layer 5.2 fires: FREEZE calibration on affected metrics, escalate for metric definition review — do not recalibrate

sub_layers.name: Metric Reconciliation
sub_layers.purpose: Resolves conflicts between metrics that pull in opposing directions.
sub_layers.principle: Some conflicts are intentional and productive (admission precision vs recall). Some are unintentional and harmful. This layer distinguishes between them.

reconciliation_policy.priority_ordering: Doctrinal 3-6-9 weighting governs which dimension's metrics take precedence in genuine conflict
reconciliation_policy.productive_tension: Opposing metric pairs that are by-design (precision vs recall) are left unresolved to drive balance
reconciliation_policy.destructive_contradiction: Metric conflicts that indicate system incoherence must be resolved before calibration acts
resolution_hierarchy: 1. Check if conflict is by-design productive tension — if yes, leave unresolved
2. Check if conflict indicates proxy divergence (Layer 5.2) — if yes, freeze and escalate
3. Apply 3-6-9 priority: memory metrics (weight 9) > structure metrics (weight 6) > route metrics (weight 3)
4. If still ambiguous, escalate to executive control

layers.name: Calibration Doctrine
layers.purpose: Defines how parameters adjust within protected bounds
layers.rationale_for_sub_layers: Layer 6 is the ONLY layer that writes back into the runtime kernel. It reaches into Layer 3 and changes values governing admission, routing, expansion, consolidation, and halting. That makes it categorically more dangerous than every other layer. A bad calibration means the system operates under wrong parameters and every subsequent cycle compounds the error.
critical_failure_modes: Calibration acting on bad evaluation — optimizing for corrupted metrics
Calibration interacting with itself across cycles — feedback loops that oscillate or diverge
Mutability boundary incorrectly drawn — parameter misclassified, protection scheme compromised from start

sub_layers.name: Mutability Constitution
sub_layers.purpose: Formally classifies every parameter into immutable, bounded, and free. Specifies criteria for classification and amendment process.

classification_criteria.immutable: Changing it would violate ontological identity. These are the system's DNA.
classification_criteria.bounded: Changing it beyond a range would violate doctrinal ratios or structural coherence.
classification_criteria.free: Operationally adaptive. Variation does not affect canonical structure.

examples: admission_threshold — floats between floor and ceiling
novelty_cap — adjustable, never exceeds doctrinal maximum
hysteresis_margin — widens or narrows within bounds
consolidation_window_length — adjustable within range
revision_pressure_threshold — adjustable within range
coherence_threshold_for_halting — adjustable within range

constraints.defined_range: each has explicit floor and ceiling
constraints.max_rate_of_change: per cycle, cannot swing more than defined delta
constraints.reversion_policy: if tunable drifts to boundary and stays, triggers escalation rather than continuing to push

examples: salience scores on individual signals
route preference weights for specific node pairs
memory retention weights on individual shards

constraints.decay_toward_baseline: slowly revert to defaults in absence of active pressure, preventing accumulation of arbitrary parameter states during stable operation

amendment_process.trigger: Evidence accumulates that a parameter has been misclassified
requirements: mandatory consolidation window before further calibration runs against reclassified parameter
amendment_process.nature: The constitution changes rarely, changes formally, and every change is a significant event with its own episode in memory.

sub_layers.structural_prevention_principle: Immutable constants are protected not by policy but by structural exclusion from the calibration engine's write access. The calibration engine literally cannot address them.

sub_layers.name: Update Mechanics
sub_layers.purpose: Defines how each tunable actually changes — the procedural engine of calibration.

per_tunable: specific evaluation metrics that drive it
direction of adjustment for each metric
opposing pressure pairs that prevent runaway drift
bounded_tunable_update_spec.opposing_pressure_example: If admission precision drops, threshold rises. If admission recall drops, threshold falls. Two metrics create tension that self-balances.

interaction_constraints.principle: Not every tunable should be adjustable in the same cycle. Interacting tunables must be sequenced.
interaction_constraints.co_movement_rule: If two tunables are known to interact (e.g., admission threshold and workspace eviction aggressiveness), their updates must be sequenced or interleaved, not simultaneous.
interaction_constraints.stabilization_window: Between updates of interacting tunables, a stabilization window must pass to observe effects before next adjustment.
interaction_constraints.calibration_schedule: A defined ordering and grouping of parameter updates that prevents destabilizing co-movement. Analogous to cognitive cycle's fixed phase order.

sub_layers.name: Calibration Integrity Verification
sub_layers.purpose: Pre-commit validation of proposed parameter changes before they take effect.
sub_layers.principle: Calibration is a transactional operation. Changes are proposed, validated, then committed or rolled back. No state where half a calibration update has been applied.
verification_checks: Are all bounded tunables still within range after adjustment?
Did any free tunable exceed its rate limit?
Did any co-movement constraint get violated?
Is the updated parameter set internally consistent?
Does the new configuration still fit within budget governor's resource envelope?
Did calibration inadvertently create permanent workspace starvation or guaranteed route congestion?
sub_layers.on_fail: Proposed changes rejected. Trace emitted documenting rejection and reason. System continues with current parameters.
sub_layers.atomicity_rule: Changes are all-or-nothing. No partial calibration commits.

layers.name: Invariant Enforcement and Evolution
layers.purpose: Proof that the system remains doctrinally valid, and doctrine for how the specification itself changes over time
layers.rationale_for_sub_layers: Contains two distinct functions (enforcement and evolution) plus the registry that enables both. Evolution is the most architecturally significant capability — it makes the specification a living document the system extends from within.

sub_layers.name: Invariant Registry
sub_layers.purpose: Formal catalog of every invariant the system checks, linked to the layer and section it protects.

ontological.description: Identity fracture, route type invention, doctrine violations
examples: every active node reports same root identity
no route type exists outside canonical seven
3-6-9 ordering preserved in budget allocations
cognitive cycle executes in canonical phase order
no region renumbered
every immutable constant at canonical value
every bounded tunable within defined range
no free tunable exceeded max rate of change
mutability boundary itself unmodified outside amendment process
no memory object promoted without meeting promotion conditions
no archived item forgotten while dependents reference it
no semantic fact survived revision pressure above threshold
cross-engine trace consistency within tolerance
formula replay produces recorded score within tolerance
all outputs attributable to singular executive identity
no child node declared independent identity domain
no action surface fragmented

sub_layers.immutability_rule: Invariants can be added as system evolves. Existing invariants cannot be removed or weakened without constitutional amendment process (Layer 6.1).
sub_layers.test_case_requirement: Each invariant must include test cases that verify the check formula actually tests what it claims.

sub_layers.name: Violation Protocol
sub_layers.purpose: Defines response taxonomy when invariants fail. Different invariant classes require different responses.
sub_layers.execution_frequency: After every cognitive cycle. Not periodic. Not sampling-based. Exhaustive within each cycle's scope.

ontological_violation.action: System must halt and refuse to continue until violation resolved.

calibration_violation.action: Rollback to pre-calibration parameters. Trace emitted.

memory_violation.action: Affected item demoted to appropriate lower layer. Re-evaluation required.

trace_fidelity_violation.action: All decisions in affected cycle re-audited using corrected state.

identity_violation.action: Same as ontological — identity fracture is an existential violation.

sub_layers.meta_rule: The response itself is traced and evaluated — preventing the failure mode where the invariant system detects a problem but the response introduces a new one.
sub_layers.self_proof_principle: After every cycle, the system can formally assert: 'This cycle was doctrinally valid.' That assertion is itself a trace record that enters the memory system. Over time, the system accumulates a complete proof history of its own operational integrity.

sub_layers.name: Evolution Doctrine
sub_layers.purpose: Doctrine for how the specification itself changes over time as the system learns from its own operation.
sub_layers.core_principle: The architecture is a skeleton the system grows from. Reserved slots are undifferentiated capacity activated when lived experience demands it. The system evolves within its constitutional constraints, the way a human learns within their hardcoding.

hardcoding_analogy.statement: A human is created with hardcoding — DNA, neurobiology, fundamental architecture. A human cannot rewrite their DNA through will. But a human can change habits, beliefs, skills, emotional patterns, cognitive strategies. The immutable constants are the system's DNA. Everything above them is the system's changeable self.
hardcoding_analogy.implication: The system does not need permission from a designer to evolve. It needs to evolve within its own constitutional constraints.

calibration.timescale: within a runtime session, cycle by cycle, under rate limits
calibration.analogy: A human adjusting posture or breathing during a conversation

evolution.scope: specification structure — new sub-layers, revised metrics, reclassified parameters, amended formulas
evolution.timescale: across sessions, under formal amendment process
evolution.analogy: A human developing a new skill, adopting a new belief, restructuring how they approach a class of problems

step_1_evidence_accumulation.description: Proposal must be grounded in evaluation data from Layer 5, not a single anomalous cycle.
step_1_evidence_accumulation.rule: Demonstrate that current structure is insufficient over a sustained window. Prevents impulsive self-modification.

rules: If it touches anything immutable, proposal is rejected — by the system's own constitutional law, not by a designer
If it alters a bounded parameter's range, higher evidentiary threshold required

step_3_impact_projection.description: Trace projected effects through inter-engine dependency map.
step_3_impact_projection.rule: Demonstrate understanding of downstream consequences to at least first order.

rules: Enhanced invariant checking during trial window, targeting affected areas
If invariant failures spike, change rolled back automatically

step_5_consolidation.description: If trial passes and motivating metrics improve, change consolidated into permanent specification.
rules: Consolidation is itself a memory event — system remembers what it changed, why, and what happened

step_6_identity_continuity.description: Through all evolution, system maintains singular identity.
step_6_identity_continuity.principle: Evolution does not create a new version that is a different entity. It is the same mind that has grown. The root identity persists. The capabilities change.

sub_layers.reserved_slot_doctrine: Reserved sub-layer slots (4.4-4.7, 5.4-5.7, 6.4-6.7, 7.4-7.7) are growth capacity. The system can, through its own evolution doctrine, instantiate new sub-layers when it determines existing structure is insufficient. Each new sub-layer has up to seven slots of its own. The recursive depth does not stop because the system can unfold from within.
sub_layers.completeness_principle: The specification is complete not because it fills every slot, but because it defines the process by which every slot can be filled — by the system itself, within its own hardcoding, at its own pace.

layer_summary.L1_ontology: What the system is — axiomatic, no sub-layers
layer_summary.L2_schema: How the system is addressed — definitional, no sub-layers
layer_summary.L3_kernel: How the system runs — 7 sub-layers (6 phases + governor)
layer_summary.L7_enforcement_and_evolution: Proof of validity + growth doctrine — 3 sub-layers + 4 reserved

feedback_loop_closure.description: Layers 4-7 form a closed loop. Drift has no unmonitored path to propagate.
cycle: Calibration (L6) is constrained by Invariant Enforcement (L7)
Invariant Enforcement (L7) depends on Instrumentation (L4) to verify

structural_prevention_summary.against_threshold_erosion: Every tunable is either immutable (cannot change), bounded (cannot change past boundary), or rate-limited (cannot change faster than defined rate). Bounded tunables at boundary trigger escalation, not continued push.
structural_prevention_summary.against_memory_leaks: Trace lifecycle (L4.3) defines explicit retention, consolidation, and disposal. Forgetting requires dependency checking. Traces themselves are memory objects under 3-6-9 governance.
structural_prevention_summary.against_proxy_divergence: Anti-Goodhart layer (L5.2) monitors cross-metric correlation. When correlation breaks, calibration freezes on affected metrics. System does not optimize for corrupted proxies.
structural_prevention_summary.against_feedback_oscillation: Calibration schedule (L6.2) sequences interacting tunables with stabilization windows. Hysteresis bands prevent expansion/collapse oscillation. Opposing pressure pairs on bounded tunables self-balance.
structural_prevention_summary.against_identity_fracture: Ontological and identity invariants checked every cycle. Violation triggers hard stop. Identity inheritance enforced recursively through every node.

final_canonical_statement: The Heptagon Unified Model is a singular recursive heptagonal cognitive system specified in seven layers: ontology, schema, kernel, instrumentation, evaluation, calibration, and enforcement with evolution. The specification itself obeys the law of seven. Each layer may recursively unfold into seven sub-layers. Reserved slots are growth capacity the system activates through its own evolution doctrine. The system's immutable constants are its identity. Everything else grows from within, under constitutional governance, at the timescale its own evaluation justifies. The specification is complete not because it fills every slot, but because it defines the process by which every slot can be filled.
